items.rationale: Building open-source zkVMs to make proving faster/simpler, scale blockchains, and bring more computation onchain; his single most active personal project area.

citations.quote: Today we're sharing our biggest open source release yet: Jolt Alpha, our zkVM built new from the ground up.
We've developed a new lookup argument called Lasso that is already 10x faster than halo2
Jolt memory usage is going to decrease massively over the next few months. And we're aiming constant+low memory

items.rationale: Believes the coming flood of AI-generated content can only be countered with cryptographic provenance, attestation graphs, and private sybil-resistant identity.

citations.quote: Every robust method to prove the origin or history of content involves cryptography and blockchains.
Crypto skeptics should explain how they think we're going to solve this problem. I don't think it can be done seriously without crypto.
cryptographic signatures, decentralized and private identities, and censorship resistant systems are all key ingredients

items.rationale: Core thesis: open permissionless networks resist centralization and let users own usernames/data, unlike closed corporate web2 networks.

citations.quote: You don't own your username — or anything else — on a corporate network.
the 'fun' is the result of permissionless innovation in an open system
released a dapp on the new River protocol (user-owned chat communities)

items.rationale: Wants trust-minimized verification (light clients verifying L1/L2 in-browser) baked into wallets and RPC providers.

citations.quote: You can run a Helios light client in the browser to verify Ethereum L1 + OP Mainnet + Base. It works right now.
Remarkable how powerful it is to give every user a cryptographic key that is linked to a secure verifiable computer (blockchain).

items.rationale: Sees payments (cheap USDC transfers bypassing ~30% platform fees) as a founding crypto goal whose pieces are finally coming together.

citations.quote: Payments has been one of the goals of crypto from the beginning. The pieces are finally coming together.
merchants could save ~30% and maintain a more direct relationship with users. The benefits of crypto start becoming very obvious.

items.rationale: Autonomous AI agents can hold wallets, pay each other and for their own compute, and respect cryptographic/consensus constraints more easily than laws.

citations.quote: AI agents can't have bank accounts, but they can have crypto wallets.
agents being able to pay each other, and even for their own compute, is what I had in mind here. It will definitely happen.
It's much easier for machines respect constraints imposed by cryptography and blockchain consensus than constraints imposed by laws

items.rationale: Views games as a perennial source of software innovation and points to a game L3 leading all chains by throughput (Mgas/s).

citations.quote: Narrative violation: #1 chain measured by Mgas/s (the correct measure for throughput) is @ProofOfPlay — by a long shot.
onchain games ... all compute constrained

items.rationale: Wants a composable rights layer so derivative/remix creativity is easy while original authors are compensated downstream.

citations.quote: Story Protocol fixes this with an onchain remix graph that makes composition and attribution natural.
deliberately allow people to borrow your voice ... Need a good IP management system.

items.rationale: Argues Ethereum must raise blob capacity and not slow down because founders pick infrastructure on present and future expected capacity; many good ideas are compute-constrained.

citations.quote: Ethereum should raise the blob target to 4 ... Ethereum cannot afford to slow down.
90% of the unfeasible but good ideas I hear are primarily limited by compute.

items.rationale: Teaches and obsesses over principled token mechanism design — faucets, sinks, network effects, whether voting is even needed.

citations.quote: When designing decentralized networks with tokens, there's so much to consider.
Why issue tokens at all? What are some common pitfalls? I talked about crafting network tokens

items.rationale: Champions fresh research-lab designs enabling trustless data markets and cheaper verification.

citations.quote: With FDE, you could set up a trustless market to buy data.
Naysayer proofs can be extremely efficient, requiring proof verification to be executed onchain only when there's an error.

items.rationale: Wants to bring zero-knowledge to Bitcoin (e.g. a VERIFY_SNARK opcode) to make it programmable and freely movable, reviving Satoshi's vision.
items.age_note: Argued mainly Sept 2023; not restated in 2024 though the related Bitcoin security/ossification concern persists.

citations.quote: adding a VERIFY_SNARK opcode to the bitcoin protocol — would make bitcoin totally programmable and free to move all over the internet.
We should bring zero knowledge back home to bitcoin.

items.rationale: Prefers 'smart accounts' (identity + ownership) over wallets as a foundation for richer automated onchain behavior.
items.age_note: Stated mid-2023, not restated since; treat as a persisted view.

citations.quote: Smart account is much better than smart wallet ... 'account' implies both identity and ownership — which is the truth.

items.rationale: His strongest, most repeated concern: speculation deters serious founders, hands ammunition to politicized regulators, and reframes crypto as a casino — damaging adoption daily.

citations.quote: we shouldn't pretend that the casino doesn't set us back. Deterring great founders and giving ammo to politicized regulators has a profound effect.
It's a memecoin when that's all there is.
fomo can use the full intellect to justify itself with elaborate stories

items.rationale: Expects most online content to soon be assumed AI-generated unless cryptographically attested — a dystopia he sees as nearly unavoidable absent crypto provenance.

citations.quote: The 'mystery meat' scenario seems unavoidable: 'where is this content from?' 'no idea but it's good'
all content is assumed generated unless accompanied by the requisite provenance/signatures/attestations/proofs

items.rationale: Closed networks block real experimentation and ownership, and naive p2p systems degrade to centralization without proper architecture.

citations.quote: Because of its network architecture, Nostr likely degrades to centralization — like Fediverse. This happens often in simple p2p systems.
No e/acc nocoiner I know adequately appreciates the ways centralization limits the speed of innovation.

items.rationale: Believes crypto founders are being debanked and politicized regulators are a real threat; pushes back on those denying it happened.

citations.quote: Why are your priors telling you this did not happen? Respectfully, they may need adjusting
giving ammo to politicized regulators has a profound effect

items.rationale: Frustrated that headline crypto metrics are flawed and don't reflect real users, developers, or revenue, distorting how progress is judged.

citations.quote: It's extremely obvious why 'active addresses' is flawed in multiple ways — yet no other measure seems to be better.
It's a very bad metric.

items.rationale: Worries Bitcoin's refusal to upgrade and its long-run (fee-based) security model threaten its survival over coming decades.

citations.quote: A side effect of bitcoin's extreme ossification is that normies believe other blockchains are also not changing or upgrading.
the security model of bitcoin needs to change if it's going to last the next few decades.

items.rationale: Argues closed models drastically limit innovation and safety; favors open, even uncensored models plus crypto-based trust tooling.

citations.quote: Closed source drastically limits innovation from startups, universities, independent researchers ... And it makes it more difficult to keep software safe.

items.rationale: Cheap, fast onchain payment rail that lets merchants bypass ~30% platform fees and own the user relationship.

citations.quote: If an app can collect payment from a mobile crypto wallet (think quick and cheap USDC transfer), merchants could save ~30%

items.rationale: Sees private, sybil-resistant proof-of-personhood as a necessary complement to trust graphs for defending networks against AI; repeatedly cites it as the model.

citations.quote: How bad does this need to get before all networks need to integrate a private proof of personhood system like @worldnetwork?
a sybil resistance mechanism, which is what Worldcoin provides

items.rationale: Appreciates Bitcoin's existence and sees latent potential if made ZK-programmable — but this excitement is tempered by ossification/security worries.
items.age_note: Endorsement is appreciative-but-qualified; he simultaneously flags ossification and an aging security model (see concerns).

citations.quote: It's incredible that we have bitcoin.
All of the things Satoshi envisioned become possible again.

items.rationale: Pure-meme/store-of-value tokens have no enterprise value and, as a category, represent the casino dynamic he thinks sets the industry back.

citations.quote: memecoins/store of value tokens like Dogecoin have no enterprise value (like gold).
It's a memecoin when that's all there is.

risk_regime.rationale: Eddy's posture is that of a long-horizon infrastructure builder: deeply, structurally bullish on crypto-the-computer (ZK, verifiable computing, open networks, payments, AI-agent rails) and willing to keep shipping open-source infra regardless of market conditions. But he is explicitly risk-OFF on speculation/memecoins and the 'casino,' which he views as net-negative. So the bullishness is conviction in fundamental technology and adoption, not in speculative price action.

notes: View synthesized from an a16z-crypto infrastructure builder, not a trader — he rarely endorses specific tokens to hold, so tokens lists are sparse and protocol/sector-weighted. Recency weighting: ZK/zkVM (Jolt), deepfake-provenance, user-owned networks, payments, AI-agent rails, and the anti-memecoin stance are all reinforced through late 2024 and treated as current. Bitcoin-ZK-programmability and smart-accounts are mid-2023 views never reversed but not restated → marked persisted. No genuine reversals detected; the one terminology shift noted is his Sept-2023 switch from 'Data Availability' to 'Data Publishing/Storage' (not surfaced as a market view). Worldcoin/World ID appears as both a system he champions and an implied token; conviction reflects the system endorsement. Bitcoin appears in both excited and concerned lists because his stance is genuinely mixed (appreciative of its existence/potential, worried about ossification and security model).
